title: nie-monero: address_for_session minor index masked to 31 bits — 2^31 UUID pairs share an address
description:
## Bug

In nie-monero/src/lib.rs address_for_session():

    let b = session_id.as_bytes();
    let minor = u32::from_le_bytes([b[0], b[1], b[2], b[3]]) & 0x7FFF_FFFF;
    self.subaddress(0, minor)

Two session UUIDs that differ only in bit 31 of their first 4 bytes map to the same minor index. For example:
- UUID with bytes[0..4] = 0x00000001 → minor = 1
- UUID with bytes[0..4] = 0x80000001 → minor = 0x80000001 & 0x7FFF_FFFF = 1 (same!)

With random UUIDs, the birthday paradox gives a 50% collision probability after approximately 2^15.5 ≈ 46,341 sessions on the same wallet. In practice, each collision means two different payment sessions share the same Monero receive address, making it impossible to attribute which session received a particular payment.

The mask was presumably added to avoid the u32::MAX minor index (Monero's max_minor is 2^32-1, so u32 max is fine), but 0x7FFF_FFFF is overly conservative and introduces unnecessary collisions.

## Fix

Remove the 0x7FFF_FFFF mask — u32 full range is valid for Monero subaddress minor indices (the Monero protocol uses u32). If there is a protocol reason for the mask, document it.
